level1_segments.summary: The head surgeon prepares the patient and performs initial cutting and drilling tasks with brief pauses in between.
The head surgeon calibrates the Mako robotic arm and manipulates it while touching the patient.
The head surgeon uses a bone saw to cut the patient's bone, interspersed with brief periods of holding instruments and touching the patient.
The head surgeon performs initial bone sawing and tissue manipulation with brief pauses.
The surgeon alternates between drilling, hammering, and touching the patient to prepare the surgical site.
The head surgeon continues with intermittent drilling and hammering before applying cement and finalizing the procedure.
The head surgeon performs hammering and cementing tasks on the patient while intermittently touching the surgical site.
The surgeon engages in a prolonged sequence of touching the patient and holding instruments, interspersed with brief pauses and a short cementing action.
The head surgeon drills the patient using a surgical instrument and then touches the site.
The head surgeon sutures the patient followed by a brief pause.

level2_segments.summary: The head surgeon prepares the patient and calibrates the robotic arm for the procedure.
The surgeon performs extensive bone cutting, sawing, and drilling to prepare the surgical site.
The head surgeon applies cement and performs final adjustments to secure the implant.
The surgeon completes final drilling and proceeds to suture the wound.

level1_segments.summary: The robotic system undergoes a comprehensive pre-surgical calibration sequence including arm status checks, homing, accuracy verification, brake testing, and angle discrepancy validation.

The surgeon performs detailed bone registration for the tibia and femur, capturing specific anatomical landmarks such as anterior, posterior, distal, and bevel surfaces to establish the surgical plan.
The robotic system initiates a new operational phase following the completion of bone registration.

level2_segments.summary: The robotic system completes its pre-surgical calibration and the surgeon performs detailed bone registration to establish the surgical plan.
The robotic system transitions to a new operational phase following the completion of bone registration.

level0_segments.description: robot setup (physical): Turn On
robot setup (physical): Initial Calibration by MPS

active_predicates: Dressing the robot, to make it sterile
level0_segments.description: robot setup (physical): Dressing the robot, to make it sterile
robot setup (physical): Install base array by nurse
robot setup (physical): Install calibration array
robot setup (physical): Remove calibration Array
robot setup (physical): Install actual Saw tip

level1_segments.summary: The robot is powered on to initiate the setup process.
Initial calibration of the robot is performed by the MPS.
The robot is dressed with sterile coverings to prepare for surgery.
The base array and calibration array are installed and subsequently removed to complete the calibration sequence.
The actual saw tip is installed onto the robot to finalize the physical setup.

level2_segments.summary: The robot is powered on and undergoes initial calibration to prepare for the surgical procedure.
The robot is dressed in sterile coverings, undergoes final calibration with array installation, and is fitted with the saw tip to complete the physical setup.

level1_segments.summary: The bone saw is held sequentially by the scrub nurse and then the assistant surgeon during a long idle period.
The head surgeon holds and actively uses the bone saw to perform cutting tasks.

level2_segments.summary: The bone saw transitions from an idle state held by support staff to active use by the head surgeon for cutting tasks.
